key_insight: The paper derives reliable a posteriori error bounds for stabilized finite element approximations of time-dependent mean field games by linking the solution error to the dual norm of the residual.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a practical way to certify and adapt FEM solutions for coupled Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman and Kolmogorov-Fokker-Planck systems in dynamic multi-agent models.
method: Prove equivalence between error norm and residual dual norm for the coupled MFG equations.
Build an a posteriori estimator from spatial residuals, temporal jump terms, and stabilization contributions.
For mass-lumped time and affine-preserving spatial stabilizations, bound stabilization terms using residual and jump estimators.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical derivation of reliable and efficient estimators, with improved local computability and local efficiency under specific stabilization choices.
watch_item: The improved estimator relies on particular stabilization assumptions, so implementations may need care when the discretization does not use mass-lumping in time or affine-preserving spatial stabilization.
paper_title: A posteriori error bounds for finite element approximations of time-dependent mean field games
